title: Paneer Paratha

steps.text: Make the dough: combine atta and salt, add lukewarm water gradually, and knead into a soft smooth dough. Cover and rest for 20 minutes.
Combine crumbled paneer, green chilli, ginger, coriander leaves, cumin seeds, red chilli powder, garam masala, amchur and salt in a bowl. Mix gently — do not over-work or the paneer becomes pasty.

Divide dough into 8 balls. Roll one into a 10 cm circle. Place 2 heaped tablespoons of paneer filling in the centre, gather the dough edges up and pinch shut. Press the stuffed ball flat and roll carefully to an 18 cm round.
Place the paratha on a medium-hot tawa and cook for 90 seconds on the first side. Flip, drizzle half a teaspoon of ghee around the edges, and cook another 60 seconds.
Flip again, add a little more ghee and press lightly with a spatula. Cook until both sides show golden patches and the paratha feels slightly firm at the edges — about 2 more minutes.
Serve hot straight from the tawa with curd, mint chutney or butter. These parathas hold their heat well in a foil-lined container for the lunchbox.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: paneer → crumbled firm tofu (press out extra moisture first); amchur → a squeeze of lemon juice; ghee → butter or oil
